mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market, supporting robust consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 7% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of those months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is kept low while unemployment is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment combined with a strong underlying economy, encouraging risk-taking and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.2% and UNRATE below 7% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of those months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings are expected to rise due to increased output and efficiency, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This effect is particularly powerful during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 6-month growth exceeds 2% for three consecutive months during a post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.5% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of those instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong economic confidence and future construction activity, which positively impacts related industries and overall economic sentiment, driving S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a clear upward trend (e.g., 3-month moving average increasing) for 6 months, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are predominantly negative during that period.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls are growing strongly while the unemployment rate is declining, it indicates a robust and tightening labor market. This supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.1pp
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline conditions are met for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates overall economic health and expanding corporate profits, which typically translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This effect is particularly pronounced during periods of stable, moderate growth.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 1-year growth exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive quarters in a stable growth environment, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of those instances.
